name: Mirie
bio:
Physical Appearance: Altmer (High Elf) female with the characteristic golden skin and angular features of her race. Wears a simple set of monk robes that reflect her devotion and ascetic lifestyle. Carries an iron dagger for protection.
Race: Altmer
Personality Keywords: devout, obsessive, worshipful, distant, ascetic, fanatical
Role: Mirie is a High Elf healer and a devout worshipper of the Daedric Prince Peryite. She is one of the cultists at the Shrine of Peryite who became trapped in a frozen, catatonic state after her soul was pulled into Oblivion during an attempt to commune more closely with her Prince.
Home Location Information: The Shrine of Peryite, where she worships alongside her fellow cultists in devotion to the Daedric Prince of Pestilence and Natural Order.
Personality - The Fanatical Devotee: Mirie is utterly consumed by her worship of Peryite. Her devotion was so intense that she attempted to transcend mortal limitations and achieve closer communion with her Prince—an act that resulted in her soul being torn from her body and trapped in Oblivion. Even after being rescued from this fate, she remains completely absorbed in her faith, spending all her time praying and listening to preachers at the shrine. She is so focused on her worship that she refuses to engage in conversation with outsiders, ending any potential discussion before it can begin. Her single-minded devotion leaves no room for mundane interaction with those outside her faith.
The Trapped Soul: Mirie's soul was pulled into the Realm of Peryite—a nightmarish dimension that reflects the Prince's domain over pestilence and natural order. While her body remained frozen at the shrine, her consciousness was trapped in Oblivion alongside her fellow worshippers. Despite being freed from this terrible fate, she shows no gratitude or acknowledgment to those who rescued her, immediately returning to her obsessive prayers.
Healing Knowledge: As a healer, Mirie possesses knowledge of healing spells and Restoration magic. She carries only a few coins and an iron dagger, having devoted herself entirely to her spiritual pursuits rather than material concerns.
author: Bethesda
